commit 755295bcaab6f366cc8eb0c3b3c4b28385804019
Author: Greg Zuro <greg@zuro.net>
Date:   Thu May 28 17:51:54 2026 -0700

    Stop auto-copying on drag release; Ctrl-Shift-C copies everywhere
    
    Auto-copy on drag release was scratchpad #91's spec but is surprising
    in practice: users expect drag = select, then a deliberate keychord =
    copy. Move the copy to Ctrl-Shift-C and leave the highlight in place
    on mouse-up so the user can see what they've selected before deciding.
    
    - Both forms' `handle_mouse` Up arms now just keep the selection
      (dropping a zero-length click selection). No clipboard write.
    - Both forms' Ctrl-Shift-C handlers extend to cover single-line
      textareas (Title / Assignee / Tags for todos, Title / Tags for
      scratchpads). For Body the wrap-aware `self.selection` is used;
      for single-line fields the textarea's native `selection_range()`
      is read. First match wins so Body takes priority when focused.
    - Doc-comments now name the WezTerm mapping: ⌘C on Mac is captured
      by WezTerm's built-in Copy and never reaches the app, so users
      who want ⌘C to act as copy can map it to Ctrl-Shift-C in their
      WezTerm config.
    
    `drag_then_release_populates_selection_and_clears_on_bare_click`
    test still passes - the post-release selection bookkeeping is the
    same shape, only the OSC 52 emit is removed from the path.
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: b882955acbff

commit faf2b8ae4bf1834499a7deea0ae20f7f8e511933
Author: Greg Zuro <greg@zuro.net>
Date:   Thu May 28 17:44:03 2026 -0700

    Write OSC 52 to the Zellij client's pty fd via /proc, not stdout
    
    The previous attempt wrote OSC 52 to the copy_command subprocess's
    stdout, which succeeded (rc=0) but didn't reach WezTerm because
    Zellij's server is daemonized: the server's stdout points at
    /dev/null, so the subprocess inherits a black-hole fd. /dev/tty
    ENXIOs for the same reason (no controlling terminal).
    
    The only fd on the system actually connected to the user's
    WezTerm-via-SSH pty is the Zellij CLIENT process's fd/1. Walk /proc,
    match `comm=zellij*` whose fd/1 readlinks to a `/dev/pts/*` device,
    and write OSC 52 to that fd. The client's pty is the user's terminal,
    so the bytes reach WezTerm directly - same path Zellij itself uses
    when it writes its render frames.
    
    The /dev/tty fallback remains for the case where /proc isn't
    available (macOS) or no zellij client fd is found.
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: b6054b0f6822

content.content:
commit d45cbd3779d4bfcfaac5ef9909bfef9e30c8df21
Author: Greg Zuro <greg@zuro.net>
Date:   Thu May 28 16:34:03 2026 -0700

    Wire Zellij's copy_command to an OSC 52 -> /dev/tty helper script
    
    Zellij's default OSC 52 path - intercept the pane's sequence, store in
    pending_clipboard_update, re-emit via ClipboardProvider::Osc52 as a
    pre-VTE instruction on the next client Render - does not actually
    land on the host clipboard in some chain configurations. The bare OSC
    52 from the user's gd3 SSH shell (before Zellij) reaches the WezTerm
    clipboard fine, so we know SSH and WezTerm are not the bottleneck;
    the Zellij re-emit is dropped somewhere between
    `add_pre_vte_instruction_to_multiple_clients` and the client stdout
    write.
    
    Bypass that whole path by setting `copy_command`. When Zellij has a
    `copy_command` configured, `ClipboardProvider::Command` is used
    instead of OSC52; Zellij spawns the command (via `Command::new`,
    splitting on single spaces - so no shell quoting allowed) and pipes
    the text on stdin.
    
    - `paths::copy_helper_script()` is the stable path
      `~/.local/share/panopt/copy-to-osc52.sh`.
    - `ensure_copy_helper_script()` writes the script + chmod 755. The
      body reads stdin, base64-encodes (Linux `-w 0` or macOS plain),
      wraps in OSC 52, writes to `/dev/tty`. `/dev/tty` is the controlling
      terminal of the Zellij client process, which is the user's WezTerm
      pty over SSH - so the OSC 52 reaches the host clipboard the same
      way the user's own `printf` test did.
    - `append_copy_command_if_unset` adds `copy_command "<path>"` to the
      generated cockpit config only when the user's base config has no
      active setting. KDL comments (`// copy_command ...`) do not count
      as "set" - the dump's commented-out examples shouldn't suppress us.
    - `render_config` takes the helper path and threads it through; the
      failure mode is a warning and the cockpit comes up regardless (the
      user just loses the OSC-52-to-clipboard path).
    
    Three unit tests cover: no setting present, user setting respected,
    commented-out lines treated as unset.
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: 602fd448b680

 crates/panopt/src/up.rs    | 123 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++--
 2 files changed, 126 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

390	fn ensure_copy_helper_script() -> Result<PathBuf> {
391	    let path = paths::copy_helper_script()?;
392	    // Body: capture stdin to a temp file, log what we got (size + content)
393	    // for diagnostic purposes, then base64-encode and write OSC 52 to
394	    // /dev/tty. The temp-file detour lets us read stdin twice (once for the
395	    // log, once for base64); without it we'd consume stdin in the encoder
396	    // and the log line would be blank.
397	    //
398	    // Linux `base64 -w 0` suppresses line wrap; macOS `base64` has no `-w`
399	    // but emits one line by default - the `tr -d '\n'` fallback covers both.
400	    // The OSC 52 terminator is `\x07` (BEL); some terminals also accept
401	    // `\x1b\\` (ST) but BEL is universal.
402	    //
403	    // The debug log lives at `~/.local/share/panopt/copy-debug.log` and
404	    // grows without bound; it's a deliberate one-off troubleshooting aid.
405	    let log_path = paths::copy_debug_log()?;
406	    let body = format!(
407	        r#"#!/bin/sh
408	# Generated by `panopt up`. Reads selection text on stdin and emits OSC
409	# 52 to the user's terminal so the host clipboard updates. Zellij
410	# invokes this as its `copy_command`.
411	#
412	# Why the /proc fd hunt: Zellij's `copy_command` subprocess is forked
413	# by the server with stdin piped, no controlling terminal
414	# (`/dev/tty` ENXIOs), and stdout inherited from the server. The
415	# server is daemonized so its stdout points at /dev/null - writes
416	# succeed but go nowhere. The only fd in the system that's actually
417	# connected to the user's WezTerm-via-SSH pty is the Zellij CLIENT
418	# process's fd/1; we find that process by walking /proc and looking
419	# for a `comm=zellij*` whose fd/1 resolves to a /dev/pts/* device.
420	LOG={log_path}
421	tmp=$(mktemp) || tmp=/tmp/panopt-copy-$$.tmp
422	cat > "$tmp"
423	{{
424	  echo "=== $(date) pid=$$ tty=$(tty 2>/dev/null) ==="
425	  echo "stdin bytes: $(wc -c < "$tmp")"
426	  printf 'stdin head:  '; head -c 200 "$tmp"; echo
427	}} >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null
428	b=$(base64 -w 0 < "$tmp" 2>/dev/null || base64 < "$tmp" | tr -d '\n')
429	echo "base64:      $b" >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null
430	target=""
431	for p in /proc/[0-9]*; do
432	  comm=$(cat "$p/comm" 2>/dev/null) || continue
433	  case "$comm" in zellij*) ;; *) continue ;; esac
434	  fd1="$p/fd/1"
435	  [ -e "$fd1" ] || continue
436	  link=$(readlink "$fd1" 2>/dev/null) || continue
437	  case "$link" in
438	    /dev/pts/*|/dev/tty*)
439	      target=$fd1
440	      echo "target pid=$(basename "$p") comm=$comm fd1=$link" >> "$LOG"
441	      break
442	      ;;
443	  esac
444	done
445	if [ -n "$target" ]; then
446	  printf '\033]52;c;%s\007' "$b" > "$target" 2>>"$LOG"
447	  echo "osc52 rc: $?" >> "$LOG"
448	else
449	  echo "osc52: no zellij client pty found, falling back to /dev/tty" >> "$LOG"
450	  printf '\033]52;c;%s\007' "$b" > /dev/tty 2>>"$LOG"
451	  echo "osc52 tty rc: $?" >> "$LOG"
452	fi
453	rm -f "$tmp"
454	"#,
455	        log_path = log_path.display(),
456	    );

The core fact: WezTerm intercepts ⌘C as its built-in Copy action which copies *WezTerm's own selection* — not anything the app inside knows about. For ⌘C to work in panopt without remapping (which is what you're saying — and that's correct, remapping ⌘C globally would break it everywhere else), WezTerm has to have a selection of its own to copy.

WezTerm does have a built-in for this that doesn't need any config: **shift+drag bypasses the app's mouse capture and makes a WezTerm-native selection**. Then ⌘C copies WezTerm's selection. This is how copy works in vim, htop, claude code, etc. without any of them implementing OSC 52.

Question for you (not asking what to do — asking what you're observing): when you drag-select right now and see the highlight, is the highlight the panopt-drawn one (style depends on the form's render), or WezTerm's normal selection (the solid inverted/blue background WezTerm always uses)? If it's the panopt highlight and no shift — the form is grabbing the mouse, WezTerm sees nothing, ⌘C copies WezTerm's empty selection (clipboard cleared, exactly what you saw). The fix is to let shift+drag pass through to WezTerm; the form just has to not steal the shift-modified mouse events.

content.text:
Let me stop and just lay out what's actually possible without remapping ⌘C — because there is a path that needs zero config and zero code change.

WezTerm has a built-in: **shift+drag** bypasses the app's mouse capture and makes a WezTerm-native selection (the same blue highlight you get in any terminal app). content.text:
You've nailed the actual limitation: WezTerm has no idea Zellij has split the screen into panes — WezTerm just sees one big screen, so its native selection runs across the whole row regardless of what's drawn on it. That's why shift+drag is unusable here.

So we have a real wall. ⌘C is handled by WezTerm before any bytes leave your Mac. There are exactly three doors:

1. **Shift+drag** — broken, as you saw (cross-pane).
2. **Globally remap ⌘C** — what you correctly rejected; breaks every app.
3. **Scoped remap, active only inside panopt.** WezTerm supports "key tables" — a remap that's only in effect when explicitly turned on. Panopt sets a WezTerm user-var (via OSC 1337) when the cockpit starts and clears it on exit; your wezterm.lua activates the panopt key table when that var is set, deactivates it when it isn't. **⌘C in bash, vim, every other app, every other WezTerm pane in another window — all completely untouched.** The remap only fires while you're in the cockpit.

That's the only path that gives you ⌘C without trashing it elsewhere. It does mean a one-time addition to wezterm.lua, but it's scoped — not the "remap basic keystrokes" you're rejecting.

Want me to wire option 3? Concretely:
- Panopt emits `\e]1337;SetUserVar=panopt_active=...\007` on cockpit up / cockpit down.
- Form handles a chord (e.g. Alt+C, which is unambiguous in standard ANSI and doesn't collide with Ctrl+C / Close).
- Provide a copy-pastable wezterm.lua snippet that: listens for the user-var, activates a `panopt` key table, and binds ⌘C in that table to `SendKey {key='c', mods='ALT'}`.

If that's not what you want, tell me what is.

Built. Confirming coverage:

**Form panes — drag-release auto-copies via OSC 52 (`crate::clip::copy_to_clipboard`):**
- Todo form: Body (wrap-aware), Title, Tags, Assignee. (Status/Priority are select buttons, no text; Comments/Blockers are list sections — click-to-focus only, no inline drag wired.)
- Scratchpad form: Body, Title, Tags.

The Up arm in both `handle_mouse` checks `self.selection` (Body) first, then falls back to the focused single-line textarea's `selection_range()`. Both paths call `crate::clip::copy_to_clipboard` which runs OSC 52 + the plugin pipe + external clipboard daemons concurrently.

**Agent/terminal panes — Zellij's `copy_on_select=true` (default) + the helper script:**
- `~/.config/panopt/cockpit-config.kdl` has `copy_command "/home/gregz/.local/share/panopt/copy-to-osc52.sh"` wired.
- On drag-release inside a Zellij-managed pane, Zellij pipes the selection text to the helper on stdin; the helper finds the Zellij client's pty via `/proc` and writes OSC 52 to it.
- Helper debug log (`~/.local/share/panopt/copy-debug.log`) confirms `osc52 rc: 0` for the writes that have happened.

**One genuine caveat for terminal/agent panes:** if the app running in the pane captures the mouse (Claude Code does via DECSET 1000), Zellij never sees the drag — so `copy_on_select` doesn't fire. For those panes you need Zellij scroll mode first (the cockpit binds it to Ctrl+Shift+S since bare Ctrl+S is reserved for the form save). I can't bypass this from the panopt side — the mouse events are physically routed to the app by Zellij before our code runs.

Test it: drag-release in a form field, then drag-release inside a plain shell pane. Both should land in your Mac clipboard. Tail `~/.local/share/panopt/copy-debug.log` if you want to see the helper log a `viewer copy_to_clipboard` line (form path) and a `stdin head:` line (Zellij→helper path).
